summary: Backend-only Batch 3B verification executed: lint/build pass, runtime regression pass, plus targeted worker-timeout probe exposing runtime stability bug.

critical.issue: When a queued/dispatched worker task times out, timeout handler removes job and rejects promise but never resets worker.busy/currentJobId. Reproduced with 1ms feature-task timeout; worker remains busy=true permanently, causing pool starvation/deadlock for subsequent tasks.

action_items: Fix timeout cleanup in WorkerPoolService.enqueue so timed-out dispatched jobs also release worker state (busy/currentJobId) and optionally terminate+respawn poisoned worker.
Add regression guard in worker pool tests for timeout->recovery behavior.
Re-run runtime regression and timeout probe after fix using isolated DATA_DIR.
critical_code_review_comments: Worker runtime currently has single-failure deadlock risk: one timeout can permanently reduce effective worker capacity to zero for that worker type.
Batch 3B dist worker path preference works under current build artifacts and real worker execution.
seed_data_creation: No persistent seed data; isolated temp DATA_DIR paths used for each run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Passing checks: yarn lint, yarn build, TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=testtoken TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_USER_IDS=1 DATA_DIR=/tmp/mafiamarkets-regression-b3b-t1b LOG_DIR=/tmp/mafiamarkets-regression-b3b-t1b/logs TEMP_DIR=/tmp/mafiamarkets-regression-b3b-t1b/tmp yarn tsx /app/tests/runtime_backend_regression.ts. Failing probe: TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=testtoken TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_USER_IDS=1 DATA_DIR=/tmp/mafiamarkets-timeout-probe LOG_DIR=/tmp/mafiamarkets-timeout-probe/logs TEMP_DIR=/tmp/mafiamarkets-timeout-probe/tmp yarn tsx /app/tests/worker_timeout_probe.ts.
rca of the issue: RCA: timeout callback in enqueue only deletes jobs map entry + queue entry and rejects promise. It does not clear owning worker wrapper state, so onWorkerMessage ignores late response because job no longer exists, leaving wrapper.busy=true/currentJobId set forever. Reproduction: invoke private enqueue('feature', payload, 1ms) after workerPool.start(); wait ~25ms; snapshot() shows feature worker busy true. Mitigation: on timeout, locate worker by currentJobId, clear busy/currentJobId, set lastError, and preferably terminate+recreate worker to avoid stale in-flight execution.
